preview_text:
The model favors Colombia heavily, assigning them a 76% win probability. DR Congo faces a steep task, rated at just 4% by the statistical forecast, while a draw stands at 20%.

The spread reflects a lopsided matchup in the model's view. Colombia's three-to-one advantage over a draw outcome suggests the forecast expects decisive play, though the 20% draw probability leaves room for a competitive, low-scoring contest. DR Congo's 4% win chance indicates the model sees them as unlikely to prevail under neutral conditions.

Model and market diverge noticeably. The sportsbook consensus lowers Colombia to 65% and raises DR Congo to 13%—a gap of roughly 11 percentage points in the underdog's favor. Polymarket prices align more closely with books than the model. The dashboard makes no claim about which estimate is more accurate; the difference may reflect information not captured in the statistical framework.
